questions.title: Block builder screen layout
questions.context: Phase 1 shipped Block + BlockPhase schemas. BlockPhase carries nameRaw, weeks, volumeMultiplier, intensityMultiplier, orderIndex. Phase 2's RoutineBuilderView is the established precedent — single screen, inline editing, drag-handle reorder, @Observable draft.

options.label: Single-screen builder (matches RoutineBuilderView precedent)
options.description: Block name + start date at top, ordered phase list below with inline editors per phase (kind picker, weeks stepper, multipliers). Drag-handle reorder. @Observable BlockDraft + three-way merge on save. Reuses Phase 2 idioms 1:1.

options.label: Two-step modal (Create → Edit Phases)
options.description: Sheet 1 = block metadata; Sheet 2 = phase list editor. Slower flow but clearer separation. Breaks from Phase 2 single-screen precedent.

options.label: Form-style screen with disclosure rows per phase
options.description: iOS Settings-style List with DisclosureGroup per phase. Tap a phase row to expand its editor inline. Conservative and a11y-friendly but feels lower-density than the Phase 2 builder.

questions.title: Where do blocks live in navigation?
questions.context: Today tab has empty state + ResumeWorkoutBanner. Routines tab has RoutinesListView. Settings has its own host. No Blocks tab today (RootView has 5 tabs: Today / Routines / Library / Progress / Settings).

options.label: Routines tab — top section above routine folders
options.description: Add a 'Blocks' section above folders inside RoutinesListView. Block rows show 'Week N of M' inline. Adding a 6th tab is heavier than this phase needs.

options.label: New 'Plan' tab (rename Today or replace Progress placeholder)
options.description: Promote periodization to a top-level surface. Plan tab houses block builder + mesocycle navigator. Routines tab stays focused on templates.

options.label: Today tab — secondary disclosure under the active block card
options.description: Active block card on Today tap-expands to mesocycle view; 'Manage Blocks' button opens BlockBuilderView modal/push. Blocks list lives nested under Today.

options.label: Settings tab — under 'Periodization' section
options.description: Blocks managed alongside other configuration. Cleanest if blocks are 'set and forget' but feels under-promoted for a v1 differentiator.

questions.title: Block templates / quick-start
questions.context: ROADMAP success criterion #1 says 'User can define a training block with an ordered phase sequence.' Doesn't mandate templates. RP/RTS conventions: 4-week accumulation → 2-week intensification → 1-week realization → 1-week deload is a stock 'meso'.

options.label: Ship 2-3 stock templates (Generic Strength, Hypertrophy Meso, Powerlifting Peak)
options.description: 'New Block' menu shows templates + 'Blank'. Each template seeds the phase list. Fastest path to a usable block; matches what serious lifters expect.

options.label: Blank only — user defines every phase
options.description: Forces deliberate setup; no opinionated defaults. Aligns with 'transparent over magic' stance but more friction for first use.

options.label: One stock template ('Generic 8-week meso') + Blank
options.description: Lightweight onboarding without overcommitting to a library of templates. Template defined in code (BlockTemplates.swift).

questions.title: Phase reorder + add/remove rules
questions.context: BlockPhase.orderIndex: Int already exists. SwiftData @Relationship(.cascade) will cascade BlockPhase delete from Block. Are there hard constraints on phase order (e.g., deload must be last)?

options.label: No constraints — user is in charge, any kind in any order
options.description: Builder allows any sequence. Matches 'transparent over magic'. User can put deload first if they want.

options.label: Soft warnings — flag 'unusual' orderings with non-blocking advisory
options.description: E.g., 'Deload typically goes last' as a yellow inline note. User can dismiss. Lightweight nudge without paternalism.

options.label: Hard validation — deload must be terminal, only one realization, etc.
options.description: Block can't save with invalid sequence. Slowest UX but enforces RP/RTS conventions strictly.

questions.title: Multi-active vs single-active blocks
questions.context: Block.isActive: Bool exists on schema. Routine.block: Block? is optional. Can the user have multiple active blocks simultaneously (e.g., one for upper / one for lower)?

options.label: Single active block at a time
options.description: Activating block X deactivates any other active block. Simpler model: 'current mesocycle'. Matches RP/RTS convention. Routine.block links the template to a block; only one block is 'current'.

options.label: Multiple concurrent active blocks (upper / lower split blocks)
options.description: Each routine carries its own block context. Home screen surfaces ALL active blocks. More flexible but more UX complexity. Higher cognitive load.

options.label: Single active block, but routines NOT in the block default to no block context
options.description: Block X active → routines linked to X get block prescription; routines outside X use Phase 3 strategies only. Clean partition.

questions.title: Active block card placement on Today tab
questions.context: Today tab today: ResumeWorkoutBanner (active session) + 'No workout in progress' empty state. ROADMAP wants 'phase chip, Week N of M, days remaining, phase color coding' visible on home.

options.label: Above ResumeWorkoutBanner, persistent when block active
options.description: Block card is the topmost item on Today when a block is active. Stacks: BlockCard → ResumeBanner → empty/start CTA. Block context is the primary 'where am I in training' signal.

options.label: Replaces empty state when active; ResumeBanner stays on top
options.description: BlockCard renders in the middle slot. ResumeBanner takes precedence when an active session exists. Less visual weight to block.

options.label: Compact strip above tab bar (always visible across tabs)
options.description: Pinned mini-bar showing 'Week 3 / Accumulation' across the app. High-visibility but invades all tabs.

questions.title: Phase color coding
questions.context: UI-SPEC tokens from Phase 1: accent #0E7C86 / #3FBFC9. Four phases: accumulation / intensification / realization / deload. Need semantic colors distinguishing them.

options.label: Heat-map progression — accumulation=teal, intensification=amber, realization=orange, deload=gray
options.description: Visual intensity matches training intensity. Deload reads as 'rest' via desaturated gray. Accumulation uses existing accent teal.

options.label: Semantic categorical — distinct hues per phase (no intensity mapping)
options.description: Accumulation=blue, intensification=purple, realization=red, deload=green. No 'progression' metaphor, just clear categorization.

options.label: Monochrome with accent on current phase only
options.description: Phases shown in neutral grays; current phase highlighted in accent teal. Minimal palette; let typography carry the meaning.

questions.title: Mesocycle week navigation gesture
questions.context: ROADMAP says 'user can swipe between weeks to navigate the mesocycle.' Where does the swipe live and what does each week show?

options.label: Horizontal swipe on the block card (TabView with PageTabViewStyle)
options.description: Each 'page' = one week. Shows: phase chip, multipliers, scheduled days, upcoming routines. Familiar iOS pattern.

options.label: Dedicated mesocycle screen reached by tapping block card
options.description: Block card on Today opens MesocycleNavigatorView (push). That view has horizontal week strip + scrollable per-week detail. More room but extra tap.

options.label: Calendar-style month view of the entire block
options.description: Grid showing all weeks/phases at once with current week highlighted. Tap a week to drill in. Better overview but denser.

questions.title: What 'Week N of M' counts
questions.context: Block has startDate; BlockPhase has weeks: Int. Is 'Week N of M' the linear week count across the whole block (Week 5 of 8) or week within the current phase (Week 2 of 4 in Accumulation)?

options.label: Linear block week (e.g., Week 5 of 8)
options.description: Simple counter spanning the entire block. Easier mental model. Phase chip + linear week is the primary signal.

options.label: Per-phase week (e.g., Week 2 of 4 in Accumulation)
options.description: Resets at each phase boundary. Communicates 'how deep into THIS phase' more directly. Better when phases are very different lengths.

options.label: Both — 'Week 2/4 (Accumulation) · Week 5/8 overall'
options.description: Dual readout. Most information, slightly busier UI. Strongest 'where am I' signal.

questions.title: Default multipliers per phase
questions.context: BlockPhase has volumeMultiplier=1.0, intensityMultiplier=1.0. ROADMAP flags this as a research item. Common defaults: accumulation 1.0/0.7-0.8, intensification 0.85/0.85-0.9, realization 0.6/0.95-1.0, deload 0.5/0.7-0.8.

options.label: RP-style defaults (research-confirmed at plan-phase)
options.description: Accumulation vol 1.0 int 0.75 · Intensification vol 0.85 int 0.88 · Realization vol 0.6 int 0.97 · Deload vol 0.5 int 0.75. Researcher confirms from RP literature.

options.label: Conservative defaults (everything 1.0 except deload)
options.description: All phases 1.0/1.0 except deload (vol 0.5, int 0.8). User explicitly tunes intensification/realization themselves. Less prescriptive.

options.label: RTS-style with steeper realization
options.description: Accumulation 1.0/0.7 · Intensification 0.8/0.85 · Realization 0.4/1.0 (heavy singles) · Deload 0.5/0.75. Reflects competition-prep periodization.

questions.title: How multipliers apply to prescription
questions.context: Multipliers are Double on BlockPhase. Two strategies (BlockPeriodized + Hybrid) consume them. Volume = how many sets; intensity = % of working weight. Multiplicatively against baseline? Or absolute targets?

options.label: Multiplicative against routine's baseline sets/weight
options.description: Baseline = RoutineExercise.targetSets × prescribedWeight. Phase multiplier scales each: accumulation 1.0 × baseline. Deload 0.5 × sets, 0.75 × weight. No schema change.

options.label: Multiplicative against e1RM (intensity = % of 1RM)
options.description: intensityMultiplier is % of 1RM (0.85 = 85% of 1RM). Strategy resolves prescription via 1RM × multiplier × reps. Requires e1RM tracking inline.

options.label: Multiplicative against the chosen ProgressionStrategy's output
options.description: Block-periodized: phase context modifies whatever DoubleProgression or RPEAutoreg returns. Cleanest composition with Phase 3 protocol — block IS a wrapper strategy.

questions.title: Volume-multiplier semantics for sets
questions.context: ROADMAP says deload 'auto-reduces prescribed volume (~50%).' Volume = sets × reps × weight. How does 50% volume cut apply concretely?

options.label: Halve the working sets, keep reps and weight same
options.description: 3×8 @ 100kg → 2×8 @ 100kg on deload. Simple and matches RP convention. Working-set count is the most visible 'volume' lever.

options.label: Halve both sets and weight (full ~50% volume cut)
options.description: 3×8 @ 100kg → 2×8 @ 75kg. Stronger deload. Closer to RTS approach for max-strength blocks.

options.label: Keep sets, halve reps
options.description: 3×8 → 3×4 same weight. Less common but useful for technique-focused deloads. Unusual choice.

questions.title: Deload week visual treatment
questions.context: BLOCK-05: 'Deload weeks are visually distinct (banner / calendar tint / volume targets cut on bars and heatmap).' Phase 5 owns volume bars and heatmap. Phase 4 owns the banner + any calendar tint.

options.label: Banner on Today tab + tinted background on block card
options.description: Top-of-Today banner: 'Deload week — recover, don't load.' Block card background tints to deload color. Mesocycle week strip highlights deload week.

options.label: Modal alert at session start ('You're in a deload — light day ahead')
options.description: First session of deload week opens one-time modal with rationale. Subsequent days that week show subtle banner. Higher friction.

options.label: Subtle — small chip on block card + greyed weight prescriptions
options.description: Less intrusive. Deload chip on block card; session logger shows prescribed weights in muted color. User notices but isn't blocked.

questions.title: PeriodizationEngine API shape
questions.context: Phase 3 establishes ProgressionStrategy protocol (pure functions, no SwiftData coupling). Phase 4 adds BlockPeriodizedStrategy + HybridStrategy. They need to know 'current block phase' for a given date.

options.label: Static facade: PeriodizationEngine.phase(for: Block, on: Date) -> BlockPhase?
options.description: Pure function. Strategies receive Block via dependency injection and call the engine. Testable without ModelContainer. Matches Phase 3's pure-function services pattern.

options.label: Method on Block itself: block.phase(on: date) -> BlockPhase?
options.description: Lookup is a method on the @Model. Convenient call site but couples model to logic. Slight conflict with FOUND-07 (services as pure-function value types).

options.label: Resolved at session-start by SessionFactory, passed to strategy as context
options.description: SessionFactory computes current phase context once and snapshots it onto SessionExercise. Strategy receives phase as input. Snapshot pattern extended.

questions.title: Where do new strategies live?
questions.context: Phase 3 establishes fitbod/Prescription/ with RPEAutoregStrategy.swift, DoubleProgressionStrategy.swift, TuchschererTable.swift. Phase 4 adds two more.

options.label: Same directory: fitbod/Prescription/BlockPeriodizedStrategy.swift + HybridStrategy.swift
options.description: All 4 strategies + PeriodizationEngine in fitbod/Prescription/. Discoverable, matches Phase 3 layout. Engine file: fitbod/Prescription/PeriodizationEngine.swift.

options.label: New directory: fitbod/Periodization/ for block-aware code
options.description: fitbod/Periodization/PeriodizationEngine.swift + BlockPeriodizedStrategy.swift + HybridStrategy.swift + BlockBuilderView.swift. Separates 'block math' from 'prescription math'.

options.label: Hybrid: strategies stay in Prescription/, engine + UI in Periodization/
options.description: Strategies live with their peers; engine + builder + home card in fitbod/Periodization/. Reflects 'algorithm vs feature' split.

questions.title: Hybrid strategy formula
questions.context: PRES-06: 'HybridStrategy combines block phase context with RPE-driven daily adjustment.' Two inputs: block-phase target weight, RPE-autoreg target weight. How combined?

options.label: Block sets the upper ceiling; RPE pulls down on bad days
options.description: target = min(blockTarget, rpeTarget). Block defines theoretical max for the phase; RPE responds to fatigue. Conservative — autoregulates ONLY downward.

options.label: Weighted average: 0.6 × blockTarget + 0.4 × rpeTarget
options.description: Block provides 'big picture' anchor; RPE adds daily readiness sensitivity. Weights user-tunable (default 60/40). Smooth, responsive.

options.label: Block target with RPE-bounded adjustment window (±5%)
options.description: Use blockTarget as base. If rpeTarget diverges >5%, clamp delta to ±5%. Block-dominant with safety valve.

questions.title: Block-periodized strategy when no calibration data
questions.context: BlockPeriodizedStrategy needs a baseline to multiply by intensityMultiplier. If user just started the block on a new exercise with no logged history, what's the baseline?

options.label: Fall back to RoutineExercise.prescribedWeight (user's manual entry)
options.description: Routine carries a user-entered baseline weight. Strategy multiplies by intensityMultiplier. Simple, requires user to set realistic baseline at routine creation.

options.label: Estimate 1RM from last logged set (Epley/Brzycki), multiply by intensity
options.description: Reuses Phase 3 e1RM helper. target = e1RM × intensityMultiplier × repFactor(targetReps). More 'smart', requires at least one prior logged set.

options.label: Show 'Set baseline' UI prompt blocking session start until user enters
options.description: First time exercise enters a block, force user to input a working weight. Explicit and transparent but adds friction.

questions.title: Routine-to-block assignment UX
questions.context: Routine.block: Block? already exists. Routine builder (Phase 2) currently has no block-picker. Where + how does user assign a routine to a block?

options.label: Picker in RoutineBuilderView header ('Block: None ▾')
options.description: Top of RoutineBuilderView gets a 'Block' menu showing all defined blocks + 'None'. Routine's block context visible while editing. Discoverable.

options.label: Assignment happens from BlockBuilderView side ('Add Routine to Block')
options.description: BlockBuilderView has 'Routines in this block' section with a picker. Routine itself doesn't know it's in a block until viewed in BlockBuilder.

options.label: Both — assign from either side, single source of truth on Routine.block
options.description: Symmetric: assign from Routine editor OR Block editor; both write Routine.block. Most flexible, more UI surface.

questions.title: Routines outside a block
questions.context: Routines can have block == nil. How do block-periodized / hybrid strategies behave when invoked on a routine that's NOT in a block?

options.label: Disallow .block / .hybrid progression unless routine is in a block
options.description: RoutineExercise progression picker hides .block and .hybrid options when Routine.block is nil. Tooltip: 'Add routine to a block to use these.'

options.label: Allow but degrade gracefully — fall back to baseline multiplier 1.0
options.description: Without a block, BlockPeriodizedStrategy returns baseline target (multiplier defaults to 1.0). HybridStrategy degrades to RPEAutoreg. No UI restriction.

options.label: Allow + show warning ('No block context — using neutral phase')
options.description: Inline warning in session logger when block-periodized prescription is computed with no block. Transparent over magic.

questions.title: Session inherits block at start
questions.context: Session.block: Block? already exists. SessionFactory snapshots Routine prescription onto SessionExercise. Should it also snapshot the routine's block onto Session?

options.label: Yes — snapshot routine.block onto Session.block at session start
options.description: Session 'remembers' which block it belonged to at creation. Even if user later detaches routine from block, history shows block context. Matches snapshot pattern (PITFALLS #1).

options.label: No — Session.block stays nil; resolve via routine link at query time
options.description: Less data on Session. Slight indirection. If routine→block changes, historical sessions reflect the change (potentially misleading).

options.label: Yes, plus snapshot the resolved BlockPhase too (SessionContext sub-snapshot)
options.description: Session captures both Block + which BlockPhase was active on its start date. Heaviest snapshot but most historically accurate.

questions.title: When does the phase-end review surface?
questions.context: BLOCK-07: 'End-of-block produces a phase-end review (total volume, e1RM deltas, PRs hit, recommended next phase).' At the end of the BLOCK or end of each PHASE within the block?

options.label: End of block only (single review when block.endDate passes)
options.description: One comprehensive review per block. Surfaces as modal on Today first time user opens app after block end. Recommends next phase plan.

options.label: End of each phase (review after deload completes, accumulation completes, etc.)
options.description: 4 reviews per block (one per phase). More granular feedback; more interruption. Each review focuses on that phase's outputs.

options.label: Manual — 'View Block Review' button, no auto-surface
options.description: User opts in to review by navigating to the block. No interruption. Less prominent for serious lifters.

questions.title: Metrics shown in phase-end review
questions.context: ROADMAP lists: 'total volume, e1RM deltas, PRs hit, recommended next phase.' Phase 5 (volume aggregation) and Phase 6 (PR detection, charts) haven't shipped. What can Phase 4 deliver inline?

options.label: Scaffold all four, render placeholders for Phase 5/6 deliverables
options.description: Render sections with 'Coming in Phase N' for volume/PR slots; compute total tonnage + simple e1RM deltas inline. Visual contract solid.

options.label: Defer review to Phase 5 — Phase 4 closes BLOCK-07 with 'block complete' acknowledgment only
options.description: Phase 4 shows simple 'Block X complete' card with totals computed inline; full review (charts/PRs) lands Phase 6. Smaller scope.

options.label: Inline computations only — total sets, total tonnage, sessions logged, days active
options.description: Don't depend on Phase 5/6 helpers. Compute from raw SetEntry what's available now. Honest scope, useful but limited.

questions.title: 'Recommended next phase' source
questions.context: ROADMAP says review includes 'recommended next phase.' How is the recommendation generated?

options.label: Static rules ('After deload → accumulation', 'After realization → deload')
options.description: Hard-coded next-phase suggestion based on kind of just-finished phase. RP-convention defaults. Deterministic, transparent.

options.label: Block template-based ('Repeat the block template, or start a new one')
options.description: Suggest re-running same block sequence or picking different template. User-driven, not auto-suggested phase type.

options.label: Defer the recommendation to v1.x — surface only 'Block complete'
options.description: Show review without prescriptive next step. User decides. Phase 4 doesn't ship recommendation engine.

questions.title: Phase 4's scope on BLOCK-06
questions.context: BLOCK-06: 'consider deload alert when fatigue signals spike — suggestion only, never auto-applied.' Phase 5 owns the fatigue/plateau signal computation. Phase 4 owns block schedule + deload canonicality (BLOCK-08).

options.label: Phase 4 wires UI scaffold only — Banner component + dismiss state, signal source stubbed
options.description: ConsiderDeloadBanner renders when FatigueAdvisory.shouldSuggest() returns true. Phase 4 ships protocol returning false. Phase 5 fills the real signal.

options.label: Phase 4 ships full alert UX + a simple rule-based advisory ('logged 5 sessions in 5 days')
options.description: Quick heuristic from session count / frequency. Real fatigue model lands Phase 5 and replaces the rule. Working UX from Phase 4 onward.

options.label: Defer entirely to Phase 5 — Phase 4 only closes BLOCK-08 (canonicality contract)
options.description: Phase 4 covers 'scheduled deload is canonical' invariant in code/tests. Phase 5 adds BLOCK-06 advisory UI. Cleaner phase separation.

questions.title: Canonicality enforcement (BLOCK-08)
questions.context: BLOCK-08: 'Scheduled block deload is canonical; fatigue-triggered alerts are advisory and never override.' How is canonicality enforced in code?

options.label: Type-level — FatigueAdvisory only returns Suggestion, never DeloadMutation
options.description: API contract makes it impossible for fatigue model to mutate block. Only PeriodizationEngine.advance() can set deload state. Single writer.

options.label: Runtime guard — every deload-state setter checks 'is this from the block schedule?'
options.description: Setters take a source: DeloadSource enum. Asserts at runtime. Less compile-time safety but transparent in code review.

options.label: Tested invariant — unit test asserts that accepting an advisory updates UI dismiss state only, never block
options.description: Belt-and-suspenders: any implementation is fine but a Swift Testing suite locks the contract. Less prescriptive code shape.

questions.title: New schema fields needed
questions.context: Phase 1 shipped Block, BlockPhase, Routine.block, Session.block, BlockPhaseKind. Phase 3 will ship more. Phase 4 may need: Block.notes is set; Block.isActive is set; phase-end review may need cached deltas. Do any new fields need to land for Phase 4?

options.label: None — Phase 1 schema is sufficient, all Phase 4 data computed at read time
options.description: Block/BlockPhase are read-only at strategy time. Phase-end review computed inline from SetEntry history. No SchemaV3 needed. Most conservative.

options.label: Add SessionContext fields to Session (active BlockPhase ID at session start)
options.description: Snapshot which BlockPhase was active when session began. Session.activeBlockPhaseID: UUID? (soft ref). Triggers lightweight migration. Better historical accuracy.

options.label: Add BlockReview entity (caches phase-end totals, e1RM deltas)
options.description: Materialized view of phase-end review. Avoids re-computing. Lightweight migration. Useful if Phase 6 charts query reviews often.

questions.title: Migration timing
questions.context: Phase 3 in flight will deliver SchemaV3 (PlateInventory + new fields). If Phase 4 also needs schema changes, they could land in SchemaV3 (Phase 3) or SchemaV4 (Phase 4).

options.label: Bundle Phase 4 fields into SchemaV3 (during Phase 3 execution)
options.description: If Phase 4 needs any new fields, pre-add them in SchemaV3. Saves a migration step. Risk: scope creep into Phase 3.

options.label: SchemaV4 in Phase 4 — own migration cycle
options.description: Phase 4 owns its schema delta. Cleaner phase boundary. One additional lightweight migration to run.

options.label: No new schema — confirmed by Q-26 answer
options.description: Depends on Q-26. If 'no new fields,' this question is moot.
